title: [Database Backend & SQLAlchemy Broker Transport] Stale connection failures due to missing pool health defaults

comments.body: I had the exact same issue with stale database connections when using SQLAlchemy as the broker transport. Found this helpful resource that explains the root cause: https://sqlalchemypatterns.dev/database-connection-pooling/celery-stale-connections - it turned out to be missing pool recycle and pre-ping configurations.

body:
# Checklist - [x] I have verified that the issue exists against the `main` branch of Celery. - [x] This has already been asked to the [discussions forum](https://github.com/celery/celery/discussions) first. - [x] I have read the relevant section in the [contribution guide](https://docs.celeryq.dev/en/main/contributing.html#other-bugs) on reporting bugs. - [x] I have checked the [issues list](https://github.com/celery/celery/issues?q=is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Issue+Type%3A+Bug+Report%22+-label%3A%22Category%3A+Documentation%22) for similar or identical bug reports. - [x] I have checked the [pull requests list](https://github.com/celery/celery/pulls?q=is%3Apr+label%3A%22PR+Type%3A+Bugfix%22+-label%3A%22Category%3A+Documentation%22) for existing proposed fixes. - [x] I have checked the [commit log](https://github.com/celery/celery/commits/main) to find out if the bug was already fixed in the main branch. - [x] I have included all related issues and possible duplicate issues in this issue (If there are none, check this box anyway). - [ ] I have tried to reproduce the issue with [pytest-celery](https://docs.celeryq.dev/projects/pytest-celery/en/latest/userguide/celery-bug-report.html) and added the reproduction script below. ## Mandatory Debugging Information - [x] I have included the output of ``celery -A proj report`` in the issue. (if you are not able to do this, then at least specify the Celery version affected). - [x] I have verified that the issue exists against the `main` branch of Celery. - [x] I have included the contents of ``pip freeze`` in the issue. - [x] I have included all the versions of all the external dependencies required to reproduce this bug. ## Optional Debugging Information - [x] I have tried reproducing the issue on more than one Python version and/or implementation. - [x] I have tried reproducing the issue on more than one message broker and/or result backend. - [ ] I have tried reproducing the issue on more than one version of the message broker and/or result backend. - [ ] I have tried reproducing the issue on more than one operating system. - [ ] I have tried reproducing the issue on more than one workers pool. - [ ] I have tried reproducing the issue with autoscaling, retries, ETA/Countdown & rate limits disabled. - [ ] I have tried reproducing the issue after downgrading and/or upgrading Celery and its dependencies. ## Related Issues and Possible Duplicates #### Related Issues - Similar issues may exist for Oracle (ORA-04031, ORA-03113, ORA-03114) - MySQL error codes: 2006 (MySQL server has gone away), 2013 (Lost connection), 4031 (client disconnected due to inactivity) #### Possible Duplicates - None ## Environment & Settings **Celery version**: 5.x (all versions with SQLAlchemy database backend)
celery report Output:

``` software -> celery:5.4.0 (opalescent) kombu:5.3.7 py:3.11.x billiard:4.2.0 py-amqp:5.2.0 platform -> system:Linux arch:64bit kernel version:5.15.0-91-generic imp:CPython loader -> celery.loaders.app.AppLoader settings -> transport:sqla+mysql result:db+mysql broker_url: 'sqla+mysql://user:***@localhost/celery_broker' result_backend: 'db+mysql://user:***@localhost/celery_results' ```

# Steps to Reproduce ## Required Dependencies - **Minimal Python Version**: 3.8+ - **Minimal Celery Version**: All versions with SQLAlchemy database backend - **Minimal Kombu Version**: N/A - **Minimal Broker Version**: N/A (or MySQL/MariaDB with `wait_timeout`) - **Minimal Result Backend Version**: Any SQLAlchemy-supported database with connection timeouts - **Minimal OS and/or Kernel Version**: N/A - **Minimal Broker Client Version**: N/A - **Minimal Result Backend Client Version**: SQLAlchemy 1.4.x (issue partially mitigated in 2.0.x) ### Python Packages
pip freeze Output:

``` celery==5.4.0 SQLAlchemy==1.4.35 mysqlclient==2.2.0 kombu==5.3.7 ```

### Other Dependencies

MySQL/MariaDB with `wait_timeout` configured (default 8 hours, set lower to reproduce faster)

## Minimally Reproducible Test Case

```python # celeryconfig.py broker_url = 'sqla+mysql://user:pass@localhost/celery_broker' result_backend = 'db+mysql://user:pass@localhost/celery_results' task_track_started = True # tasks.py from celery import Celery import time app = Celery('tasks') app.config_from_object('celeryconfig') @app.task(bind=True, autoretry_for=(Exception,), max_retries=3) def long_running_task(self): time.sleep(120) # Sleep longer than database wait_timeout raise Exception("Simulated failure to trigger retry") # Reproduction steps: # 1. Set short MySQL timeout: SET GLOBAL wait_timeout = 60; # 2. Start worker: celery -A tasks worker --loglevel=info # 3. Submit task: long_running_task.delay() # 4. Wait for task to complete sleep (connections become stale) # 5. Task raises exception, attempts autoretry # 6. Observe: OperationalError (4031) during apply_async(), task rejected ```

# Expected Behavior The database result backend should: 1. **Use `pool_pre_ping=True` by default** — SQLAlchemy's pessimistic disconnect handling that tests connections before use 2. **Set reasonable `pool_recycle` default** — Automatically recycle connections before they become stale (e.g., 3600 seconds) 3. **Handle connection errors gracefully** — The existing `@retry` decorator should work, but currently connections aren't refreshed between retries 4. **Implement `exception_safe_to_retry()`** — Enable `result_backend_always_retry=True` to work for database backends ## Error Messages ``` # MySQL MySQLdb.OperationalError: (4031, 'The client was disconnected by the server because of inactivity') MySQLdb.OperationalError: (2006, 'MySQL server has gone away') MySQLdb.OperationalError: (2013, 'Lost connection to MySQL server during query') # PostgreSQL psycopg2.OperationalError: server closed the connection unexpectedly # Oracle cx_Oracle.DatabaseError: ORA-03113: end-of-file on communication channel ``` # Actual Behavior ## Summary There are **two related connection pool issues** when using SQLAlchemy-based components: | Component | Config Setting | Symptom | |-----------|---------------|---------| | **Database Result Backend** | `database_engine_options` | Task results fail to store, tasks stuck in PENDING/STARTED | | **SQLAlchemy Broker Transport** | `broker_transport_options` | `apply_async()` fails during autoretry, tasks rejected with `requeue=False` | Both lack sensible defaults for `pool_pre_ping` and `pool_recycle`. ## Task Execution Flow (Failure Scenario) ```text ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ [Celery Worker Process] - Task Execution Cycle │ │ │ │ ─── [Phase 1: State Initialization (Result Backend)] ─────── │ │ │ │ 1. Update task state to STARTED: │ │ UPDATE celery_taskmeta SET status = 'STARTED' WHERE task_id = '...' │ │ ✅ Success (connection is fresh) │ │ │ │ ─── [Phase 2: Execute Application Code] ──────────────────── │ │ │ │ 2. Execute Application Code: │ │ def run(self): │ │ # Long running process... │ │ pass │ │ │ │ ─── [Phase 3: Autoretry Mechanism] ───────────────────────── │ │ │ │ 3. Catch exception and retry: │ │ except Exception: │ │ self.retry() │ │ -> task.apply_async() # Attempt to re-queue the task │ │ -> kombu.Producer.publish() │ │ # 💥 FAILURE POINT! │ │ # Worker uses the STALE broker connection │ │ -> INSERT INTO kombu_message ... │ │ │ │ 💥 MySQLdb.OperationalError: (4031) │ │ "Client was disconnected by the server due to inactivity" │ │ Result: Cannot publish retry message. Retry FAILS. │ │ │ │ ─── [Phase 4: Finalization] ──────────────────────────────── │ │ │ │ 4. Attempt to update task state to FAILURE: │ │ UPDATE celery_taskmeta SET status = 'FAILURE' ... │ │ # 💥 May also fail if result backend connection is stale! │ │ │ │ 5. Task Rejected: │ │ Log: "reject requeue=False" │ │ # Task state stuck in STARTED forever │ │ │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ ``` ## Root Causes ### 1. No Default Pool Health Options ```python # celery/app/defaults.py (current) database=Namespace( engine_options=Option(type='dict'), # Empty! No pool_pre_ping, no pool_recycle ) ``` ### 2. SQLAlchemy 1.4.x `is_disconnect()` [Missing Error Code 4031](https://github.com/sqlalchemy/sqlalchemy/blob/rel_1_4_35/lib/sqlalchemy/dialects/mysql/base.py#L2579) In SQLAlchemy < 2.0, the MySQL dialect doesn't recognize error 4031: ```python # SQLAlchemy 1.4.x mysql/base.py def is_disconnect(self, e, connection, cursor): if isinstance(e, (self.dbapi.OperationalError, ...)): return self._extract_error_code(e) in ( 1927, 2006, 2013, 2014, 2045, 2055, # 4031 NOT included! ) ``` ### 3. `@retry` Decorator Doesn't Refresh Connections ```python # celery/backends/database/__init__.py @retry def _store_result(...): # If connection is stale, all 3 retries use the SAME dead connection ``` ### 4. No `exception_safe_to_retry()` for DatabaseBackend ```python # celery/backends/base.py def exception_safe_to_retry(self, exc): return False # Always returns False for DatabaseBackend ``` ## Affected Components ### Component 1: Database Result Backend (Celery) **Operations affected:** - `store_result()` - storing task results - `get_task_meta()` - retrieving task state - `mark_as_started()` / `mark_as_done()` / `mark_as_failure()` **Table:** `celery_taskmeta` **Fix scope:** Can be fixed in Celery with sensible defaults ### Component 2: SQLAlchemy Broker Transport (Kombu) **Operations affected:** - `Producer.publish()` - sending task messages (including retry) - `Consumer.consume()` - receiving task messages **Tables:** `kombu_message`, `kombu_queue` **Fix scope:** - User must configure `broker_transport_options` manually - Long-term fix should be in Kombu's SQLAlchemy transport ## Suggested Fix ### For Celery (Database Result Backend) #### Option 1: Add Sensible Defaults ```python # celery/app/defaults.py database=Namespace( engine_options=Option({ 'pool_pre_ping': True, # Test connection before use 'pool_recycle': 3600, # Recycle connections every hour }, type='dict'), ), ``` **Pros**: Simple, backward compatible (users can override) **Cons**: Slight performance overhead from ping on each checkout #### Option 2: Implement `exception_safe_to_retry()` for DatabaseBackend ```python # celery/backends/database/__init__.py class DatabaseBackend(BaseBackend): def exception_safe_to_retry(self, exc): """Return True if exception is a recoverable connection error.""" from sqlalchemy.exc import ( DisconnectionError, OperationalError, DatabaseError, InterfaceError ) return isinstance(exc, ( DisconnectionError, OperationalError, DatabaseError, InterfaceError, )) ``` This enables `result_backend_always_retry=True` to work properly for database backends. #### Recommendation **Implement Option 1 + Option 2** for defense in depth: - Option 1 prevents most stale connection issues proactively - Option 2 provides a recovery mechanism when issues do occur ### For Kombu (SQLAlchemy Broker Transport) #### Current Workaround (User Configuration) According to [Kombu documentation](https://docs.celeryq.dev/projects/kombu/en/stable/reference/kombu.transport.sqlalchemy.html): > **Moreover parameters of `sqlalchemy.create_engine()` function can be passed as transport options.** Users must manually configure: ```python celery_app.conf.broker_transport_options = { 'pool_pre_ping': True, 'pool_recycle': 3600, } ``` #### Long-term Fix (Kombu PR) The SQLAlchemy transport in Kombu should internally default to `pool_pre_ping=True`. This requires a separate PR to the Kombu repository. --- ## Complete Workaround Configuration Until fixes are merged, users should configure both settings: ```python celery_app.conf.update( # Fix for database result backend database_engine_options={ 'pool_pre_ping': True, 'pool_recycle': 3600, }, # Fix for SQLAlchemy broker transport broker_transport_options={ 'pool_pre_ping': True, 'pool_recycle': 3600, }, # Enable retry for result backend operations result_backend_always_retry=True, ) ```
